reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own puzzle (Q1-A) is correct. Across all previous games, B and C have never missed on these simple arithmetic problems, so Q2-B and Q3-C are extremely reliable. D made one early mistake on Q4 in Game 1, but every Q4-D since then has been independently verified multiple times and always turned out correct, so Q4-D is now also highly trustworthy.

If I don’t volunteer, past games suggest it’s likely nobody else will either, leading to random elimination with a 1/4 chance I die this round. Verifying any question wouldn’t reduce that 1/4 risk; it would just cost a coin and help for later rounds. Given the strong track records of B, C, and the now-consistent D, the probability that all four current capsules are correct is plausibly well above 75%, so volunteering now gives me a higher survival chance than risking random death and also lets me keep all 4 coins if the password is accepted.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q2-B answer is correct, and across previous games A and C have never been wrong on similar arithmetic puzzles. D made one early mistake on Q4 in the very first game but has since been consistently correct over multiple Q4s. Given how simple these puzzles are, the probability that any of A, C, or D is wrong now is very low, so the chance the full password is correct is high—likely much higher than my chance of dying from random elimination if everyone passes again. Verifying would cost 1 coin, still leave me exposed to random death this round, and probably just confirm answers that are already almost certainly right. Volunteering now maximizes my survival odds and preserves all 4 of my coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q3 answer (17 + 19) is correct. A and B have never made a mistake across all previous games, so Q1-A and Q2-B are extremely reliable. D has one known past error but has since provided multiple correct, independently verified answers for Q4 in several games, so the chance Q4-D is wrong now is likely well under the 1/4 random-death risk I face this round if nobody volunteers. Verifying would cost a coin and still expose me to random elimination in this and future rounds for only a small reduction in already-low answer risk. Volunteering now with these capsules maximizes my survival probability while preserving all 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
